related_requirements.requirement: The system must be scalable to handle an increase in the number of users and data volume.
related_requirements.explanation: The microservices architecture allows services to scale independently, supporting the requirement to handle a 50% workload increase without compromising performance.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must have an uptime of 99.9% and be able to recover from failures in less than 1 hour.
related_requirements.explanation: By decoupling services (Security, System, Data), a failure in one service does not necessarily bring down the entire system, aiding in fault tolerance and recovery.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be easy to maintain and update with clear and complete documentation.
related_requirements.explanation: Independent services allow for easier updates and maintenance of specific functionalities without affecting the whole application, reducing maintenance costs and time.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be compatible with common web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and mobile devices.
related_requirements.explanation: The separation of client (Next.js/React frontend) and server (API/Database) ensures the interface can be rendered across various devices and browsers while logic remains centralized.

related_requirements.requirement: The user interface must be intuitive and easy to use for non-technical users.
related_requirements.explanation: The client-side rendering (CSR) and server-side rendering (SSR) capabilities of the framework used within this architecture optimize performance and user experience.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must protect user information through secure authentication.
related_requirements.explanation: Traefik manages HTTP/HTTPS traffic, enforcing port 443 (HTTPS) to encrypt data transmission between clients and servers, ensuring privacy and security.

related_requirements.requirement: Products/routes/company profiles can only be edited and accessed by that company.
related_requirements.explanation: Traefik acts as a reverse proxy and router, managing access rules and directing traffic securely to internal services based on defined configurations.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must have an uptime of 99.9%.
related_requirements.explanation: Traefik functions as a load balancer, distributing traffic among multiple instances to improve availability and response capacity.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be available in Spanish and English.
related_requirements.explanation: The System Service integrates libraries like 'next-intl' to manage internationalization, allowing dynamic switching between languages based on URL routes.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be compatible with common web browsers and mobile devices.
related_requirements.explanation: Next.js renders the application using React, which is designed to work across different browsers and responsive layouts for mobile/tablet views.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must save all images of all products/users.
related_requirements.explanation: The System Service handles the upload logic and communicates with external storage (Amazon S3) to persist image files.

related_requirements.requirement: Products/routes/company profiles can only be edited and accessed by that company.
related_requirements.explanation: The relational database structure enforces data integrity and relationships (Foreign Keys) ensuring data ownership and access control rules are stored and validated.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be scalable to handle an increase in the number of users and data volume.
related_requirements.explanation: PostgreSQL is a robust relational database capable of handling large datasets and concurrent connections required for scaling.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be easy to maintain and update.
related_requirements.explanation: Containerization packages the application and dependencies together, simplifying deployment and updates across different environments (Dev, Pre, Prod).

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be able to recover from failures in less than 1 hour.
related_requirements.explanation: Docker allows for quick recreation and restarting of services in case of failure, facilitating disaster recovery processes.

related_requirements.requirement: Every route must have an associated budget (which can be 0) which the user must be able to pay via the system when returning the product.
related_requirements.explanation: Stripe is integrated specifically to handle payment processing for route budgets and subscription plan changes.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must have an affiliation/plans system for companies (Free, Basic, Premium).
related_requirements.explanation: Stripe manages the subscription logic and payments associated with the three different company plans.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must save all images of all products/users.
related_requirements.explanation: Amazon S3 is utilized as the cloud storage solution to store and retrieve the image files uploaded by users.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be easy to maintain and update with clear and complete documentation.
related_requirements.explanation: Automated pipelines for building, testing, and deploying reduce manual effort and ensure consistent updates, supporting maintainability goals.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be easy to maintain and update.
related_requirements.explanation: Building the UI with independent and reusable components encapsulates logic and styles, making code easier to manage, test, and update in isolation.

related_requirements.requirement: The user interface must be intuitive and easy to use.
related_requirements.explanation: Reusable components ensure consistency across the application, contributing to a uniform and intuitive user experience.

related_requirements.requirement: The user interface must be intuitive and easy to use.
related_requirements.explanation: Used with event listeners to notify components of state changes (e.g., form inputs, filters), ensuring the UI reacts immediately to user actions for a smooth experience.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be scalable to handle an increase in the number of users and data volume.
related_requirements.explanation: Ensuring only one instance of the Prisma Client exists prevents resource exhaustion and connection overhead, optimizing database interaction performance under load.

related_requirements.requirement: A company must be able to modify its profile/public page at any time.
related_requirements.explanation: Server Actions allow client components to execute server-side logic (like database updates) directly upon user interaction without writing full API endpoints, streamlining the modification process.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must protect user information through secure authentication.
related_requirements.explanation: Server Actions run on the server side, allowing secure authentication checks (using auth()) before executing sensitive database mutations.
